title: Yang Zhilin at GTC2026 Reveals: Moon's Shadow Has Replaced Adam, Full Attention, and Residual Connections, All Open Source
excerpt: The foundation of large model training is being quietly shaken by a Chinese company. In the speech "How We Scaled Kimi K2.5" at GTC2026, Moonshot CEO Yang Zhili
content_html:
The foundation of large model training is being quietly shaken by a Chinese company. In the speech "How We Scaled Kimi K2.5" at GTC2026, Moonshot CEO Yang Zhilin did not just talk about K2.5 as a product, but laid out a clear understanding the company has had over the past year: when open-source models approach closed-source frontiers, there's another path besides continuing to increase parameters and computing power — re-implementing each of the three fundamental components used in the Transformer era for nearly a decade. Optimizer Adam (proposed in 2014), attention mechanism (2017), and residual connection (2015). Moonshot has provided alternative solutions for each of these foundational components, and all are open-sourced.

Yang Zhilin broke down the entire technical roadmap into three directions: making each token more valuable, making longer context truly useful, and enabling multiple agents to collaborate simultaneously. In addition, he also introduced two important developments: how visual training can enhance text capabilities, and Moonshot’s newly announced next-generation architecture, Attention Residue.

First, the most pressing wall: high-quality data is running out. There is already limited valuable text on the internet, and as models grow larger, the amount of data needed increases, and everyone will eventually hit a data wall. Since it's hard to double the data, Moonshot's approach is to let the model learn more from the same data. The solution is MuonClip, which replaces the Adam optimizer that has been used for over a decade. Based on the Muon optimizer, it tries to keep different directions of information independent during parameter updates, reducing repetition and waste, thus improving the efficiency of data usage. Experimental results show that with the same amount of data, MuonClip's training effect is close to doubling the data volume, which lowers the training cost and raises the model's capability ceiling. Yang Zhilin gave an analogy: if you have 50 trillion high-quality tokens, doubling the efficiency equals having another 50 trillion tokens. In today's context where high-quality data is becoming increasingly scarce, such efficiency improvements are more critical than simply adding computing power.

However, Muon has a problem: when the model scales to trillions of parameters, the values in the attention layer can become unstable, with the maximum logit suddenly spiking above 1000, while the normal range is usually between 50 and 100. If the values continue to expand, the training curve may diverge, and the entire training process could collapse. To address this, Moonshot designed QK-Clip, which checks the maximum logit of each attention head in real time during forward computation. If it exceeds the safe range, it synchronously scales down the Q and K projections to bring the values back under control. It doesn't change the convergence effect, only maintains numerical stability. With QK-Clip, Muon was successfully scaled to trillions of parameters, trained on over 15 trillion tokens, and no loss spikes occurred during the entire process.

The second direction is making long context truly useful. Yang Zhilin cited experiments by Jared Kaplan and others from the Scaling Law paper: LSTM cannot improve beyond a certain length, but Transformers perform better with longer context and rarely show saturation. This characteristic becomes particularly important in the Agent era — complex tasks may last days or even weeks, and the model must remember what it has done, the results obtained, and which directions have failed. If long context fails to continuously provide effective information, the task chain is likely to break. The problem is that the computational cost of standard full attention grows quadratically with the context length. Increasing the length by ten times can increase the cost by a hundredfold, making training and inference costs extremely high at a million tokens. Moonshot's solution is Kimi Linear, which features a linear attention mechanism called KDA (Kimi Delta Attention). It enables the model to learn which information should be retained long-term and which should be quickly forgotten. Traditional linear attention uses a single global decay coefficient, like a universal forget button for all content; KDA splits this decay coefficient into multiple parts, allowing different information channels to have different forgetting speeds. Channels with slow decay retain long-distance information, while those with fast decay quickly make space for new content. In practice, Kimi Linear mixes linear attention layers and full attention layers in a 3:1 ratio. Yang Zhilin claims this is the first architecture that comprehensively outperforms full attention in short context, long input, and long output tasks. When context extends to millions of tokens or more, the efficiency advantage becomes even more apparent.

The third direction is evolving from one agent to a team of agents, which Moonshot calls Agent Swarm. Its structure resembles a company: a main agent acts as the CEO, responsible for understanding the goal, breaking down tasks, and assigning them to multiple sub-agents. Sub-agents can take roles like researchers, programmers, data analysts, and fact-checkers. Once completed, the main agent summarizes the results. The greatest value is transforming serial tasks into parallel ones. Previously, a single agent would sequentially search, read, analyze, code, and verify. Now, dozens or even hundreds of agents can work simultaneously. Training such collaboration is not easy. Moonshot designed three types of rewards: instance-based rewards encourage the main agent to create more parallel sub-tasks and avoid reverting to serial processing; completion rewards require sub-tasks to be genuinely completed, preventing the creation of empty tasks for reward inflation; and final result rewards determine whether the task was actually solved. The weights of these rewards dynamically change during training, focusing on task decomposition and parallelization in the early stages and shifting to final results in later stages. From demonstrations, Agent Swarm can simultaneously download and read hundreds of information sources for research, parallelly write different chapters of a hundred-page literature review, and simultaneously analyze ten datasets. When Kimi K2.5 was released, it supported up to 100 agents working in parallel, with tasks executing up to 1,500 steps. The K2.6 version released in April increased the parallel limit to 300 agents.

An unexpected gain emerged in the fusion of vision and text. The key difference between K2.5 and its predecessor K2 is the use of early fusion training: many open-source multimodal models previously used late fusion, first training a language model with around 20 trillion text tokens, then adding vision capabilities with about 2 trillion multimodal tokens. K2.5, however, mixed visual and text data from the very beginning, training on about 15 trillion mixed tokens on top of the K2 text base. This led to two exciting results for Yang Zhilin. First, training only on visual tasks can also improve text reasoning: the research team only let the model count, recognize images, and answer visual questions, without adding math or programming training, yet the text reasoning ability improved. In other words, the model improved its thinking ability while learning to see. Second, in reverse, if the text base is strong enough, the model might not even need specific visual SFT data — K2.5 adopted a zero-vision SFT approach, using only pure text for all supervised fine-tuning data, and then gained visual capabilities through joint reinforcement learning of text and vision. Ultimately, it achieved near state-of-the-art performance on visual tasks. Yang Zhilin believes this bidirectional transfer comes from early fusion: when text and vision enter the same representation space, the ability learned in one modality has a chance to transfer to another. This is the foundation of K2.5's ability to write code based on images.

At the end of the speech, Yang Zhilin introduced Moonshot's newly released research, Attention Residue, published on March 15th, just two days before the speech. Residual connections, proposed by He Kaiming et al. in 2015, are a fundamental technique in deep networks and now a standard component of Transformers. They allow each layer to process the upper layer's result while maintaining a direct channel for information transmission, enabling stable training of deep models. Yang Zhilin referenced Ilya Sutskever's view, interpreting residual connections as LSTM rotated 90 degrees — while LSTM transmits information along the time dimension, residual connections transmit it along the depth dimension. Since Transformers have replaced LSTM's cyclic function on the time dimension with attention, can the depth dimension also be similarly replaced? Attention Residue is the outcome of this idea: standard residuals mainly use the output of the previous layer, while it allows the current layer to access all previous layer outputs, then uses attention to decide which historical information to retain and which to ignore, letting the model actively select information from the entire computational history instead of passively receiving the upper layer's result. To control costs, the block version Block Attention Residual is used, where every 16 layers form a block — within blocks, standard residuals are still used, and attention residuals are applied between blocks. Experiments show that about 8 blocks can capture most of the benefits. It brings a 24% improvement in Token efficiency. According to Yang Zhilin's algorithm, a 24% efficiency gain on 50 trillion Tokens equals an additional 12 trillion Tokens. This improvement is especially evident in tests such as GPQA, MATH, and HumanEval, which focus on reasoning, mathematics, and programming.

sentences.en: The foundation of large model training is being quietly shaken by a Chinese company.
sentences.zh: 大模型训练的根基正在被一家中国公司悄然动摇。

sentences.en: In the speech "How We Scaled Kimi K2.5" at GTC2026, Moonshot CEO Yang Zhilin did not just talk about K2.5 as a product, but laid out a clear understanding the company has had over the past year: when open-source models approach closed-source frontiers, there's another path besides continuing to increase parameters and computing power — re-implementing each of the three fundamental components used in the Transformer era for nearly a decade.
sentences.zh: 在 GTC2026 的《How We Scaled Kimi K2.5》演讲中，Moonshot CEO 杨志林不仅谈论了 K2.5 作为一个产品，还阐述了公司过去一年的清晰认识：当开源模型逼近闭源前沿时，除了继续增加参数和计算能力之外，还有另一条路——重新实现 Transformer 时代近十年来使用的三个基本组件。

sentences.en: Optimizer Adam (proposed in 2014), attention mechanism (2017), and residual connection (2015).
sentences.zh: 优化器 Adam（2014 年提出）、注意力机制（2017 年）和残差连接（2015 年）。

sentences.en: Moonshot has provided alternative solutions for each of these foundational components, and all are open-sourced.
sentences.zh: Moonshot 为每个基础组件提供了替代解决方案，并且全部都是开源的。

blocks.html_en: Yang Zhilin broke down the entire technical roadmap into three directions: making each token more valuable, making longer context truly useful, and enabling multiple agents to collaborate simultaneously. In addition, he also introduced two important developments: how visual training can enhance text capabilities, and Moonshot’s newly announced next-generation architecture, Attention Residue.

sentences.en: Yang Zhilin broke down the entire technical roadmap into three directions: making each token more valuable, making longer context truly useful, and enabling multiple agents to collaborate simultaneously.
sentences.zh: 杨志林将整个技术路线图分解为三个方向：让每个代币更有价值、让更长的上下文真正有用、让多个代理能够同时协作。

sentences.en: In addition, he also introduced two important developments: how visual training can enhance text capabilities, and Moonshot’s newly announced next-generation architecture, Attention Residue.
sentences.zh: 此外，他还介绍了两个重要进展：视觉训练如何增强文本能力，以及Moonshot新公布的下一代架构Attention Residue。

sentences.en: First, the most pressing wall: high-quality data is running out.
sentences.zh: 首先，最紧迫的墙：高质量数据所剩无几。

sentences.en: There is already limited valuable text on the internet, and as models grow larger, the amount of data needed increases, and everyone will eventually hit a data wall.
sentences.zh: 互联网上有价值的文本本来就有限，随着模型变得越来越大，所需的数据量也随之增加，每个人最终都会遇到数据墙。

sentences.en: Since it's hard to double the data, Moonshot's approach is to let the model learn more from the same data.
sentences.zh: 由于数据很难翻倍，Moonshot 的做法是让模型从相同的数据中学到更多的东西。

sentences.en: The solution is MuonClip, which replaces the Adam optimizer that has been used for over a decade.
sentences.zh: 解决方案是 MuonClip，它取代了已经使用了十多年的 Adam 优化器。

sentences.en: Based on the Muon optimizer, it tries to keep different directions of information independent during parameter updates, reducing repetition and waste, thus improving the efficiency of data usage.
sentences.zh: 基于Muon优化器，在参数更新时尽量保持不同方向的信息独立，减少重复和浪费，从而提高数据使用效率。

sentences.en: Experimental results show that with the same amount of data, MuonClip's training effect is close to doubling the data volume, which lowers the training cost and raises the model's capability ceiling.
sentences.zh: 实验结果表明，在相同数据量的情况下，MuonClip的训练效果接近数据量的一倍，降低了训练成本，提高了模型的能力上限。

sentences.en: Yang Zhilin gave an analogy: if you have 50 trillion high-quality tokens, doubling the efficiency equals having another 50 trillion tokens.
sentences.zh: 杨志林打了个比方：如果你有50万亿优质代币，那么效率提高一倍就等于再拥有50万亿代币。

sentences.en: In today's context where high-quality data is becoming increasingly scarce, such efficiency improvements are more critical than simply adding computing power.
sentences.zh: 在当今高质量数据日益稀缺的背景下，这种效率的提高比简单地增加计算能力更为重要。

sentences.zh: 然而，Muon 存在一个问题：当模型扩展到数万亿个参数时，注意力层中的值可能会变得不稳定，最大 logit 突然飙升到 1000 以上，而正常范围通常在 50 到 100 之间。

sentences.en: If the values continue to expand, the training curve may diverge, and the entire training process could collapse.
sentences.zh: 如果这些值继续扩大，训练曲线可能会发散，整个训练过程可能会崩溃。

sentences.en: To address this, Moonshot designed QK-Clip, which checks the maximum logit of each attention head in real time during forward computation.
sentences.zh: 为了解决这个问题，Moonshot 设计了 ​​QK-Clip，它在前向计算过程中实时检查每个注意力头的最大 logit。

sentences.en: If it exceeds the safe range, it synchronously scales down the Q and K projections to bring the values back under control.
sentences.zh: 如果超出安全范围，它会同步缩小 Q 和 K 投影，使值重新受到控制。

sentences.en: It doesn't change the convergence effect, only maintains numerical stability.
sentences.zh: 它不会改变收敛效果，仅保持数值稳定性。

sentences.en: With QK-Clip, Muon was successfully scaled to trillions of parameters, trained on over 15 trillion tokens, and no loss spikes occurred during the entire process.
sentences.zh: 通过 QK-Clip，Muon 成功扩展到数万亿个参数，在超过 15 万亿个代币上进行训练，并且在整个过程中没有出现损失峰值。

sentences.en: The second direction is making long context truly useful.
sentences.zh: 第二个方向是让长上下文真正有用。

sentences.en: Yang Zhilin cited experiments by Jared Kaplan and others from the Scaling Law paper: LSTM cannot improve beyond a certain length, but Transformers perform better with longer context and rarely show saturation.

sentences.en: This characteristic becomes particularly important in the Agent era — complex tasks may last days or even weeks, and the model must remember what it has done, the results obtained, and which directions have failed.
sentences.zh: 这个特性在Agent时代变得尤为重要——复杂的任务可能会持续几天甚至几周，模型必须记住它做了什么、得到的结果以及哪些方向失败了。

sentences.en: If long context fails to continuously provide effective information, the task chain is likely to break.
sentences.zh: 如果长上下文无法持续提供有效信息，任务链很可能会断裂。

sentences.en: The problem is that the computational cost of standard full attention grows quadratically with the context length.
sentences.zh: 问题在于标准全注意力的计算成本随着上下文长度呈二次方增长。

sentences.en: Increasing the length by ten times can increase the cost by a hundredfold, making training and inference costs extremely high at a million tokens.
sentences.zh: 长度增加十倍会使成本增加一百倍，使得训练和推理成本极高，达到一百万代币。

sentences.en: Moonshot's solution is Kimi Linear, which features a linear attention mechanism called KDA (Kimi Delta Attention).
sentences.zh: Moonshot 的解决方案是 Kimi Linear，它具有称为 KDA（Kimi Delta Attention）的线性注意力机制。

sentences.en: It enables the model to learn which information should be retained long-term and which should be quickly forgotten.
sentences.zh: 它使模型能够学习哪些信息应该长期保留，哪些信息应该很快忘记。

sentences.en: Traditional linear attention uses a single global decay coefficient, like a universal forget button for all content; KDA splits this decay coefficient into multiple parts, allowing different information channels to have different forgetting speeds.
sentences.zh: 传统的线性注意力使用单个全局衰减系数，就像所有内容的通用忘记按钮； KDA将这个衰减系数分成多个部分，让不同的信息通道有不同的遗忘速度。

sentences.en: Channels with slow decay retain long-distance information, while those with fast decay quickly make space for new content.
sentences.zh: 衰减较慢的通道会保留长距离信息，而衰减较快的通道会迅速为新内容腾出空间。

sentences.en: In practice, Kimi Linear mixes linear attention layers and full attention layers in a 3:1 ratio.
sentences.zh: 在实践中，Kimi Linear 以 3:1 的比例混合了线性注意力层和全注意力层。

sentences.en: Yang Zhilin claims this is the first architecture that comprehensively outperforms full attention in short context, long input, and long output tasks.
sentences.zh: 杨志林声称这是第一个在短上下文、长输入和长输出任务中全面优于全注意力的架构。

sentences.en: When context extends to millions of tokens or more, the efficiency advantage becomes even more apparent.
sentences.zh: 当上下文扩展到数百万个令牌或更多时，效率优势变得更加明显。

blocks.html_en: The third direction is evolving from one agent to a team of agents, which Moonshot calls Agent Swarm. Its structure resembles a company: a main agent acts as the CEO, responsible for understanding the goal, breaking down tasks, and assigning them to multiple sub-agents. Sub-agents can take roles like researchers, programmers, data analysts, and fact-checkers. Once completed, the main agent summarizes the results. The greatest value is transforming serial tasks into parallel ones. Previously, a single agent would sequentially search, read, analyze, code, and verify. Now, dozens or even hundreds of agents can work simultaneously. Training such collaboration is not easy. Moonshot designed three types of rewards: instance-based rewards encourage the main agent to create more parallel sub-tasks and avoid reverting to serial processing; completion rewards require sub-tasks to be genuinely completed, preventing the creation of empty tasks for reward inflation; and final result rewards determine whether the task was actually solved. The weights of these rewards dynamically change during training, focusing on task decomposition and parallelization in the early stages and shifting to final results in later stages. From demonstrations, Agent Swarm can simultaneously download and read hundreds of information sources for research, parallelly write different chapters of a hundred-page literature review, and simultaneously analyze ten datasets. When Kimi K2.5 was released, it supported up to 100 agents working in parallel, with tasks executing up to 1,500 steps. The K2.6 version released in April increased the parallel limit to 300 agents.

sentences.en: The third direction is evolving from one agent to a team of agents, which Moonshot calls Agent Swarm.
sentences.zh: 第三个方向是从一个代理演变为一组代理，Moonshot 将其称为 Agent Swarm。

sentences.en: Its structure resembles a company: a main agent acts as the CEO, responsible for understanding the goal, breaking down tasks, and assigning them to multiple sub-agents.
sentences.zh: 它的结构类似于一个公司：一个主代理充当首席执行官，负责了解目标、分解任务并将其分配给多个子代理。

sentences.en: Sub-agents can take roles like researchers, programmers, data analysts, and fact-checkers.
sentences.zh: 子代理可以扮演研究人员、程序员、数据分析师和事实检查员等角色。

sentences.en: Once completed, the main agent summarizes the results.
sentences.zh: 完成后，主代理会总结结果。

sentences.en: The greatest value is transforming serial tasks into parallel ones.
sentences.zh: 最大的价值是将串行任务转变为并行任务。

sentences.en: Previously, a single agent would sequentially search, read, analyze, code, and verify.
sentences.zh: 以前，单个代理会依次搜索、读取、分析、编码和验证。

sentences.en: Now, dozens or even hundreds of agents can work simultaneously.
sentences.zh: 现在，数十个甚至数百个代理可以同时工作。

sentences.en: Training such collaboration is not easy.
sentences.zh: 训练这种协作并不容易。

sentences.en: Moonshot designed three types of rewards: instance-based rewards encourage the main agent to create more parallel sub-tasks and avoid reverting to serial processing; completion rewards require sub-tasks to be genuinely completed, preventing the creation of empty tasks for reward inflation; and final result rewards determine whether the task was actually solved.
sentences.zh: Moonshot 设计了三种类型的奖励：基于实例的奖励鼓励主代理创建更多并行子任务并避免恢复为串行处理；完成奖励要求子任务真实完成，防止创建空任务导致奖励膨胀；最终的结果奖励决定了任务是否真正得到解决。

sentences.en: The weights of these rewards dynamically change during training, focusing on task decomposition and parallelization in the early stages and shifting to final results in later stages.
sentences.zh: 这些奖励的权重在训练过程中动态变化，早期侧重于任务分解和并行化，后期转向最终结果。

sentences.en: From demonstrations, Agent Swarm can simultaneously download and read hundreds of information sources for research, parallelly write different chapters of a hundred-page literature review, and simultaneously analyze ten datasets.
sentences.zh: 从演示来看，Agent Swarm 可以同时下载和阅读数百个信息源进行研究，并行撰写一百页文献综述的不同章节，并同时分析十个数据集。

sentences.en: When Kimi K2.5 was released, it supported up to 100 agents working in parallel, with tasks executing up to 1,500 steps.
sentences.zh: Kimi K2.5 发布时，支持多达 100 个代理并行工作，任务执行多达 1,500 个步骤。

sentences.en: The K2.6 version released in April increased the parallel limit to 300 agents.
sentences.zh: 4月份发布的K2.6版本将并行限制提高到300个代理。

blocks.html_en: An unexpected gain emerged in the fusion of vision and text. The key difference between K2.5 and its predecessor K2 is the use of early fusion training: many open-source multimodal models previously used late fusion, first training a language model with around 20 trillion text tokens, then adding vision capabilities with about 2 trillion multimodal tokens. K2.5, however, mixed visual and text data from the very beginning, training on about 15 trillion mixed tokens on top of the K2 text base. This led to two exciting results for Yang Zhilin. First, training only on visual tasks can also improve text reasoning: the research team only let the model count, recognize images, and answer visual questions, without adding math or programming training, yet the text reasoning ability improved. In other words, the model improved its thinking ability while learning to see. Second, in reverse, if the text base is strong enough, the model might not even need specific visual SFT data — K2.5 adopted a zero-vision SFT approach, using only pure text for all supervised fine-tuning data, and then gained visual capabilities through joint reinforcement learning of text and vision. Ultimately, it achieved near state-of-the-art performance on visual tasks. Yang Zhilin believes this bidirectional transfer comes from early fusion: when text and vision enter the same representation space, the ability learned in one modality has a chance to transfer to another. This is the foundation of K2.5's ability to write code based on images.

sentences.en: An unexpected gain emerged in the fusion of vision and text.
sentences.zh: 视觉与文字的融合出现了意想不到的收获。

sentences.en: The key difference between K2.5 and its predecessor K2 is the use of early fusion training: many open-source multimodal models previously used late fusion, first training a language model with around 20 trillion text tokens, then adding vision capabilities with about 2 trillion multimodal tokens.
sentences.zh: K2.5与其前身K2的主要区别在于早期融合训练的使用：许多开源多模态模型以前使用后期融合，首先用大约20万亿个文本标记训练语言模型，然后用大约2万亿个多模态标记添加视觉能力。

sentences.en: K2.5, however, mixed visual and text data from the very beginning, training on about 15 trillion mixed tokens on top of the K2 text base.
sentences.zh: 然而，K2.5 从一开始就混合了视觉和文本数据，在 K2 文本库之上对约 15 万亿个混合标记进行训练。

sentences.en: This led to two exciting results for Yang Zhilin.
sentences.zh: 这给杨之琳带来了两个令人兴奋的结果。

sentences.en: First, training only on visual tasks can also improve text reasoning: the research team only let the model count, recognize images, and answer visual questions, without adding math or programming training, yet the text reasoning ability improved.
sentences.zh: 首先，仅进行视觉任务训练也能提高文本推理能力：研究团队只让模型计数、识别图像、回答视觉问题，没有增加数学或编程训练，文本推理能力却得到了提高。

sentences.en: In other words, the model improved its thinking ability while learning to see.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，模型在学习观察的同时提高了思维能力。

sentences.en: Second, in reverse, if the text base is strong enough, the model might not even need specific visual SFT data — K2.5 adopted a zero-vision SFT approach, using only pure text for all supervised fine-tuning data, and then gained visual capabilities through joint reinforcement learning of text and vision.
sentences.zh: 其次，反过来，如果文本基础足够强大，模型甚至可能不需要特定的视觉SFT数据——K2.5采用了零视觉SFT方法，仅使用纯文本进行所有监督微调数据，然后通过文本和视觉的联合强化学习获得视觉能力。

sentences.en: Ultimately, it achieved near state-of-the-art performance on visual tasks.
sentences.zh: 最终，它在视觉任务上实现了近乎最先进的性能。

sentences.en: Yang Zhilin believes this bidirectional transfer comes from early fusion: when text and vision enter the same representation space, the ability learned in one modality has a chance to transfer to another.
sentences.zh: 杨志林认为，这种双向迁移来自于早期的融合：当文本和视觉进入同一个表征空间时，在一种模态中学到的能力就有机会迁移到另一种模态中。

sentences.en: This is the foundation of K2.5's ability to write code based on images.
sentences.zh: 这是K2.5能够基于图像编写代码的基础。

blocks.html_en: At the end of the speech, Yang Zhilin introduced Moonshot's newly released research, Attention Residue, published on March 15th, just two days before the speech. Residual connections, proposed by He Kaiming et al. in 2015, are a fundamental technique in deep networks and now a standard component of Transformers. They allow each layer to process the upper layer's result while maintaining a direct channel for information transmission, enabling stable training of deep models. Yang Zhilin referenced Ilya Sutskever's view, interpreting residual connections as LSTM rotated 90 degrees — while LSTM transmits information along the time dimension, residual connections transmit it along the depth dimension. Since Transformers have replaced LSTM's cyclic function on the time dimension with attention, can the depth dimension also be similarly replaced? Attention Residue is the outcome of this idea: standard residuals mainly use the output of the previous layer, while it allows the current layer to access all previous layer outputs, then uses attention to decide which historical information to retain and which to ignore, letting the model actively select information from the entire computational history instead of passively receiving the upper layer's result. To control costs, the block version Block Attention Residual is used, where every 16 layers form a block — within blocks, standard residuals are still used, and attention residuals are applied between blocks. Experiments show that about 8 blocks can capture most of the benefits. It brings a 24% improvement in Token efficiency. According to Yang Zhilin's algorithm, a 24% efficiency gain on 50 trillion Tokens equals an additional 12 trillion Tokens. This improvement is especially evident in tests such as GPQA, MATH, and HumanEval, which focus on reasoning, mathematics, and programming.

sentences.en: At the end of the speech, Yang Zhilin introduced Moonshot's newly released research, Attention Residue, published on March 15th, just two days before the speech.
sentences.zh: 演讲最后，杨志林介绍了Moonshot最新发布的研究成果《Attention Residue》，该研究发表于3月15日，就在演讲前两天。

sentences.en: Residual connections, proposed by He Kaiming et al. in 2015, are a fundamental technique in deep networks and now a standard component of Transformers.
sentences.zh: 残差连接，由何凯明等人提出。 2015 年，它是深度网络的一项基本技术，现在是 Transformers 的标准组件。

sentences.en: They allow each layer to process the upper layer's result while maintaining a direct channel for information transmission, enabling stable training of deep models.
sentences.zh: 它们允许每一层处理上层的结果，同时保持信息传输的直接通道，从而实现深度模型的稳定训练。

sentences.en: Yang Zhilin referenced Ilya Sutskever's view, interpreting residual connections as LSTM rotated 90 degrees — while LSTM transmits information along the time dimension, residual connections transmit it along the depth dimension.
sentences.zh: 杨之琳引用了 Ilya Sutskever 的观点，将残差连接解释为 LSTM 旋转了 90 度——LSTM 沿着时间维度传递信息，残差连接沿着深度维度传递信息。

sentences.en: Since Transformers have replaced LSTM's cyclic function on the time dimension with attention, can the depth dimension also be similarly replaced?
sentences.zh: 既然 Transformers 在时间维度上用 Attention 替代了 LSTM 的循环函数，那么深度维度是否也可以类似替代呢？

sentences.en: Attention Residue is the outcome of this idea: standard residuals mainly use the output of the previous layer, while it allows the current layer to access all previous layer outputs, then uses attention to decide which historical information to retain and which to ignore, letting the model actively select information from the entire computational history instead of passively receiving the upper layer's result.
sentences.zh: Attention Residue就是这个想法的产物：标准残差主要使用前一层的输出，同时它允许当前层访问所有前一层的输出，然后使用注意力来决定保留哪些历史信息，忽略哪些历史信息，让模型主动从整个计算历史中选择信息，而不是被动地接收上层的结果。

sentences.en: To control costs, the block version Block Attention Residual is used, where every 16 layers form a block — within blocks, standard residuals are still used, and attention residuals are applied between blocks.
sentences.zh: 为了控制成本，使用了块版本Block Attention Residual，其中每16层形成一个块——在块内，仍然使用标准残差，并且在块之间应用注意力残差。

sentences.en: Experiments show that about 8 blocks can capture most of the benefits.
sentences.zh: 实验表明，大约 8 个区块就可以获取大部分收益。

sentences.en: It brings a 24% improvement in Token efficiency.
sentences.zh: 带来了 Token 效率 24% 的提升。

sentences.en: According to Yang Zhilin's algorithm, a 24% efficiency gain on 50 trillion Tokens equals an additional 12 trillion Tokens.
sentences.zh: 根据杨志林的算法，50万亿Token的效率提升24%相当于额外增加了12万亿Token。

sentences.en: This improvement is especially evident in tests such as GPQA, MATH, and HumanEval, which focus on reasoning, mathematics, and programming.
sentences.zh: 这种改进在 GPQA、MATH 和 HumanEval 等侧重于推理、数学和编程的测试中尤其明显。

content_html_zh:
大模型训练的根基正在被一家中国公司悄然动摇。 在 GTC2026 的《How We Scaled Kimi K2.5》演讲中，Moonshot CEO 杨志林不仅谈论了 K2.5 作为一个产品，还阐述了公司过去一年的清晰认识：当开源模型逼近闭源前沿时，除了继续增加参数和计算能力之外，还有另一条路——重新实现 Transformer 时代近十年来使用的三个基本组件。 优化器 Adam（2014 年提出）、注意力机制（2017 年）和残差连接（2015 年）。 Moonshot 为每个基础组件提供了替代解决方案，并且全部都是开源的。

杨志林将整个技术路线图分解为三个方向：让每个代币更有价值、让更长的上下文真正有用、让多个代理能够同时协作。 此外，他还介绍了两个重要进展：视觉训练如何增强文本能力，以及Moonshot新公布的下一代架构Attention Residue。

首先，最紧迫的墙：高质量数据所剩无几。 互联网上有价值的文本本来就有限，随着模型变得越来越大，所需的数据量也随之增加，每个人最终都会遇到数据墙。 由于数据很难翻倍，Moonshot 的做法是让模型从相同的数据中学到更多的东西。 解决方案是 MuonClip，它取代了已经使用了十多年的 Adam 优化器。 基于Muon优化器，在参数更新时尽量保持不同方向的信息独立，减少重复和浪费，从而提高数据使用效率。 实验结果表明，在相同数据量的情况下，MuonClip的训练效果接近数据量的一倍，降低了训练成本，提高了模型的能力上限。 杨志林打了个比方：如果你有50万亿优质代币，那么效率提高一倍就等于再拥有50万亿代币。 在当今高质量数据日益稀缺的背景下，这种效率的提高比简单地增加计算能力更为重要。

然而，Muon 存在一个问题：当模型扩展到数万亿个参数时，注意力层中的值可能会变得不稳定，最大 logit 突然飙升到 1000 以上，而正常范围通常在 50 到 100 之间。 如果这些值继续扩大，训练曲线可能会发散，整个训练过程可能会崩溃。 为了解决这个问题，Moonshot 设计了 ​​QK-Clip，它在前向计算过程中实时检查每个注意力头的最大 logit。 如果超出安全范围，它会同步缩小 Q 和 K 投影，使值重新受到控制。 它不会改变收敛效果，仅保持数值稳定性。 通过 QK-Clip，Muon 成功扩展到数万亿个参数，在超过 15 万亿个代币上进行训练，并且在整个过程中没有出现损失峰值。

第二个方向是让长上下文真正有用。 杨志林引用了 Scaling Law 论文中 Jared Kaplan 等人的实验：LSTM 无法改进超过一定长度，但 Transformers 在更长的上下文中表现更好，并且很少出现饱和。 这个特性在Agent时代变得尤为重要——复杂的任务可能会持续几天甚至几周，模型必须记住它做了什么、得到的结果以及哪些方向失败了。 如果长上下文无法持续提供有效信息，任务链很可能会断裂。 问题在于标准全注意力的计算成本随着上下文长度呈二次方增长。 长度增加十倍会使成本增加一百倍，使得训练和推理成本极高，达到一百万代币。 Moonshot 的解决方案是 Kimi Linear，它具有称为 KDA（Kimi Delta Attention）的线性注意力机制。 它使模型能够学习哪些信息应该长期保留，哪些信息应该很快忘记。 传统的线性注意力使用单个全局衰减系数，就像所有内容的通用忘记按钮； KDA将这个衰减系数分成多个部分，让不同的信息通道有不同的遗忘速度。 衰减较慢的通道会保留长距离信息，而衰减较快的通道会迅速为新内容腾出空间。 在实践中，Kimi Linear 以 3:1 的比例混合了线性注意力层和全注意力层。 杨志林声称这是第一个在短上下文、长输入和长输出任务中全面优于全注意力的架构。 当上下文扩展到数百万个令牌或更多时，效率优势变得更加明显。

第三个方向是从一个代理演变为一组代理，Moonshot 将其称为 Agent Swarm。 它的结构类似于一个公司：一个主代理充当首席执行官，负责了解目标、分解任务并将其分配给多个子代理。 子代理可以扮演研究人员、程序员、数据分析师和事实检查员等角色。 完成后，主代理会总结结果。 最大的价值是将串行任务转变为并行任务。 以前，单个代理会依次搜索、读取、分析、编码和验证。 现在，数十个甚至数百个代理可以同时工作。 训练这种协作并不容易。 Moonshot 设计了三种类型的奖励：基于实例的奖励鼓励主代理创建更多并行子任务并避免恢复为串行处理；完成奖励要求子任务真实完成，防止创建空任务导致奖励膨胀；最终的结果奖励决定了任务是否真正得到解决。 这些奖励的权重在训练过程中动态变化，早期侧重于任务分解和并行化，后期转向最终结果。 从演示来看，Agent Swarm 可以同时下载和阅读数百个信息源进行研究，并行撰写一百页文献综述的不同章节，并同时分析十个数据集。 Kimi K2.5 发布时，支持多达 100 个代理并行工作，任务执行多达 1,500 个步骤。 4月份发布的K2.6版本将并行限制提高到300个代理。

视觉与文字的融合出现了意想不到的收获。 K2.5与其前身K2的主要区别在于早期融合训练的使用：许多开源多模态模型以前使用后期融合，首先用大约20万亿个文本标记训练语言模型，然后用大约2万亿个多模态标记添加视觉能力。 然而，K2.5 从一开始就混合了视觉和文本数据，在 K2 文本库之上对约 15 万亿个混合标记进行训练。 这给杨之琳带来了两个令人兴奋的结果。 首先，仅进行视觉任务训练也能提高文本推理能力：研究团队只让模型计数、识别图像、回答视觉问题，没有增加数学或编程训练，文本推理能力却得到了提高。 换句话说，模型在学习观察的同时提高了思维能力。 其次，反过来，如果文本基础足够强大，模型甚至可能不需要特定的视觉SFT数据——K2.5采用了零视觉SFT方法，仅使用纯文本进行所有监督微调数据，然后通过文本和视觉的联合强化学习获得视觉能力。 最终，它在视觉任务上实现了近乎最先进的性能。 杨志林认为，这种双向迁移来自于早期的融合：当文本和视觉进入同一个表征空间时，在一种模态中学到的能力就有机会迁移到另一种模态中。 这是K2.5能够基于图像编写代码的基础。

演讲最后，杨志林介绍了Moonshot最新发布的研究成果《Attention Residue》，该研究发表于3月15日，就在演讲前两天。 残差连接，由何凯明等人提出。 2015 年，它是深度网络的一项基本技术，现在是 Transformers 的标准组件。 它们允许每一层处理上层的结果，同时保持信息传输的直接通道，从而实现深度模型的稳定训练。 杨之琳引用了 Ilya Sutskever 的观点，将残差连接解释为 LSTM 旋转了 90 度——LSTM 沿着时间维度传递信息，残差连接沿着深度维度传递信息。 既然 Transformers 在时间维度上用 Attention 替代了 LSTM 的循环函数，那么深度维度是否也可以类似替代呢？ Attention Residue就是这个想法的产物：标准残差主要使用前一层的输出，同时它允许当前层访问所有前一层的输出，然后使用注意力来决定保留哪些历史信息，忽略哪些历史信息，让模型主动从整个计算历史中选择信息，而不是被动地接收上层的结果。 为了控制成本，使用了块版本Block Attention Residual，其中每16层形成一个块——在块内，仍然使用标准残差，并且在块之间应用注意力残差。 实验表明，大约 8 个区块就可以获取大部分收益。 带来了 Token 效率 24% 的提升。 根据杨志林的算法，50万亿Token的效率提升24%相当于额外增加了12万亿Token。 这种改进在 GPQA、MATH 和 HumanEval 等侧重于推理、数学和编程的测试中尤其明显。
